v01.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 2
v01.description: Condition for Case 2 uses 'or' with missing parentheses, effectively flipping logic so Case 2 can be true without all conjuncts.

v02.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 2
v02.description: Case 2 uses 'and ... and ... or ...' without parentheses, causing Case 2 to be true when 'Live vaccine was administered in the last 4 weeks' regardless of other conjuncts.

v03.define: Client is not due for MCV0
v03.description: Top-level aggregation mixes 'and'/'or' without parentheses, changing precedence from the L2 'or across cases' into a different boolean condition.

v04.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 4
v04.description: Case 4 checks 'MCV0 was not administered' instead of 'MCV0 was administered' (polarity reversal).

v05.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 2
v05.description: Case 2 checks 'MCV0 was administered' but L2 requires 'MCV0 was not administered' for Case 2.

v06.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 1
v06.description: Case 1 uses 'Client's age is less than 9 months' but L2 requires 'less than 6 months'.

v07.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 3
v07.description: Case 3 uses 'Client's age is more than or equal to 12 months' but L2 requires '>= 9 months'.

v08.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 2
v08.description: Case 2 omits the 'MCV0 was not administered' conjunct; it only requires age between 6 and 9 months and live vaccine in last 4 weeks.

v09.description: Consider logic requires MCV0 not administered, age between 6-9 months, and No live vaccine in last 4 weeks, but Case 2/Case 3 output coverage differs (Case 2/3 guidance expects Case 3 text while code changes Consider/Case3 boundary); indicates an inconsistency in Case3 threshold/coverage.

v12.description: Test assertion uses 'Patient.id != 'Measles37.3'' for Case 2 expectations, which diverges from the deterministic L2-aligned test cases (logic inconsistency in guidance/define mapping).

v23.define: Client is not due for MCV0 Case 3
v23.description: Case 3 guidance block indicates case 3 threshold 'more than 9 months', but code uses '>= 9 months' (inconsistent boundary text vs predicate; L2 expects 'more than or equal to 9 months' with matching guidance).
